info.title: ValarAI API
info.description: ValarAI provides OpenAI-compatible Responses and Chat Completions endpoints, plus an Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint. This reference documents the currently supported subset of fields.

tags.name: Batches API
tags.description: Submit and manage batches of requests.

RequestMetadata.description: Optional string metadata. completion_window controls scheduling; completion_webhook/webhook_token configure completion webhooks.

completion_window.description: [Completion window](/completion-windows) (i.e. latency tier) for the request. Support matrix is available on the [Pricing](/pricing) page. When omitted, defaults to `standard` if that window is supported for the model; otherwise `flex` for async requests when the model supports it, and `asap` in all other cases. See [default behavior](/completion-windows#default-behavior).

ResponseInputImagePart.description: Image content part. Image input is supported only on multimodal models; see the Models page.

image_url.description: Public http(s) URL or a base64 data URI (data:<media-type>;base64,<data>).

ResponseInput.description: Text input, plus image input (input_image) on multimodal models. Audio, files, and item references are not currently supported.

prompt_cache_key.description: Optional routing hint for prompt-prefix cache locality. Requests with the same key are preferentially routed to maximize cache hit rates.

ChatImageContentPart.description: Image content part. Image input is supported only on multimodal models; see the Models page.

url.description: Public http(s) URL or a base64 data URI.

stream.description: When true, the response is returned as a Server-Sent Events stream of chat.completion.chunk objects instead of a single JSON response.

stream_options.description: Options that apply when stream is true.

include_usage.description: When true, emit a final chunk with token usage for the full request before the [DONE] terminator.

ChatCompletionChunk.description: One Server-Sent Events chunk emitted when stream: true. The streamed response is a sequence of these chat.completion.chunk objects, each sent as an SSE data: line and terminated by a final data: [DONE] line.

AnthropicImageBlock.description: Image content block. Image input is supported only on multimodal models; see the Models page.

media_type.description: Image MIME type, e.g. image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, image/gif.

url.description: Public http(s) URL of the image.

tools.description: Accepted for Anthropic SDK compatibility but currently ignored. The model does not receive tools and cannot call them.

tool_choice.description: Accepted for Anthropic SDK compatibility but currently ignored.

custom_id.description: A unique identifier for this request within the batch. Must be lowercase alphanumeric and can include hyphens and underscores. Min 1 character, max 64 characters.

params.description: The request parameters (same fields as a Responses API request body).

endpoint.description: The API endpoint to use for all requests in the batch. Currently only /v1/responses is supported.

requests.description: The list of requests in the batch. Min 1, max 100,000 requests. Total request body must not exceed 256 MB.

label.description: An optional label for the batch. Must be alphanumeric (hyphens and underscores allowed), max 128 characters.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Makes the request retry-safe. ValarAI stores a reservation keyed by (organization, API key, Idempotency-Key); retrying with the same value returns the previously stored response instead of re-running inference. Keys are capped at 255 characters. See [Idempotent Requests](/idempotency) for full semantics.

AnthropicIdempotencyKey.name: anthropic-idempotency-key
AnthropicIdempotencyKey.description: Anthropic-compatible idempotency header. Same semantics as Idempotency-Key; used on the /messages endpoint to match Anthropic's conventions.

post.description: Creates an OpenAI Responses API task. Returns 202 when background=true, otherwise returns 200 after completion.

value.input: Explain the key ideas behind transformer architectures.

post.description: OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions endpoint. Supports streaming via stream: true, which returns a Server-Sent Events stream of chat.completion.chunk objects.

200.description: Chat completion. Returns a single JSON object by default, or a Server-Sent Events stream of chat.completion.chunk objects when stream: true (terminated by a final data: [DONE] line).

post.description: Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint. Streaming is not currently supported.

messages.content: Give me a short launch checklist for an API integration.

post.description: Submit a batch of requests for asynchronous processing.

request_counts.description: Total number of requests in the batch.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the batch was created.

label.description: The label for the batch, if provided.

parameters.name: after_id
parameters.description: Return batches created after this batch ID.

parameters.name: before_id
parameters.description: Return batches created before this batch ID.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of batches to return. Min 1, defaults to 25, max 200.

request_counts.description: Total number of requests in the batch.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the batch was created.

label.description: The label for the batch, if provided.

get.description: Retrieve the current status of a batch.

200.description: Returns the current status of a batch.

request_counts.description: Total number of requests in the batch.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the batch was created.

label.description: The label for the batch, if provided.

request_status.description: A map of custom_id to its current status.

get.description: Retrieve the result of a specific request within a batch by its custom_id.

parameters.name: custom_id
parameters.description: The custom_id of the request within the batch.

200.description: The response object for the given request.
